assertions.title: The user can disconnect and reconnect without recreating the host

steps.action: Open the session tab and connect the saved SSH fixture host
steps.expected: A live session opens for the existing host
steps.observed: The visible session flow connected to 10.0.2.2:3122 and rendered remote proof text.

steps.action: Tap Disconnect from the active session
steps.expected: The session should move to a truthful disconnected state
steps.observed: The UI showed Disconnected., disabled input, and kept the same saved host entry available.

steps.action: Reconnect using the same saved host entry
steps.expected: A fresh session should reconnect without recreating the host
steps.observed: The same host row reconnected and produced a fresh successful shell proof without recreating the host.

evidence.network: SSH fixture reachable at 10.0.2.2:3122 from emulator (adb readiness check fixture_exit:0)

assertions.title: Repeated connect taps do not create duplicate live sessions

steps.action: Start connecting a slow scripted host
steps.expected: The session should enter one connecting state
steps.observed: The UI showed Connecting… with a single visible disconnect control.

steps.action: Attempt to interact again while connect is in progress
steps.expected: Duplicate connects should be prevented
steps.observed: The connect control was disabled during the in-flight attempt, so duplicate taps were blocked.

steps.action: Inspect controller call counts after canceling the attempt
steps.expected: Only one live session attempt should have been created
steps.observed: The test finished with connectCalls == 1 and completedConnections == 0, confirming no duplicate live session was created.

evidence.network: No HTTP calls; scripted session controller recorded exactly one connect attempt while the UI kept the connect action disabled.

assertions.title: A hanging connection attempt can be canceled without leaving zombie state behind

steps.action: Begin a real SSH fixture connection until the trust/cancelable connecting state appears
steps.expected: The session should show an in-progress connection
steps.observed: The fixture session reached CONNECTING with a pending trust decision for 10.0.2.2:3122.

steps.action: Cancel/disconnect before the connection completes
steps.expected: The app should return to a clean non-connecting state
steps.observed: The session moved to DISCONNECTED with status Connection canceled. and no pending trust prompt.

steps.action: Wait for late completion or zombie resurrection
steps.expected: No hidden session should appear after canceling
steps.observed: After an additional settle delay the state remained DISCONNECTED with empty transcript and no saved trust.

evidence.network: SSH fixture reachable at 10.0.2.2:3122 from emulator (adb readiness check fixture_exit:0)

assertions.title: Rotating or backgrounding during connection setup resolves to one truthful final state

steps.action: Inspect available runnable lifecycle coverage
steps.expected: A real rotation/background validation should exist
steps.observed: The available executable suite only recreates/hides the session composable during connect; it does not rotate or background/resume the real app while connecting to the live fixture.

evidence.network: SSH fixture reachable at 10.0.2.2:3122 from emulator (adb readiness check fixture_exit:0)

assertions.issues: Blocked because the current runnable validation surface does not perform a real rotation or background/resume flow during connection setup on the shared emulator; the available composable hide/show recreation proof is not equivalent enough to claim this lifecycle assertion.

assertions.title: Typed input executes on the remote shell and resulting output is rendered in order

steps.action: Inspect runnable terminal-input coverage against the live fixture
steps.expected: A visible typed-input flow should send text from the user input surface to the remote shell
steps.observed: The live-fixture suite sends commands through coordinator.sendText/sendCommandDirectly and validates remote output, but it does not exercise visible user typing through the shipped terminal input control.

evidence.network: SSH fixture reachable at 10.0.2.2:3122 from emulator (adb readiness check fixture_exit:0)

assertions.issues: Blocked because the available executable proof confirms remote command execution but not the full visible typed-input path required by this assertion.

assertions.title: Long or multiline output remains readable and scrollback preserves earlier transcript

steps.action: Inspect runnable scrollback coverage
steps.expected: A long-output flow should be validated against the real SSH target
steps.observed: The current suite proves scrollback UI behavior with a scripted controller, but not with long or multiline output coming from the real SSH fixture target named by the contract.

evidence.network: SSH fixture reachable at 10.0.2.2:3122 from emulator (adb readiness check fixture_exit:0)

assertions.issues: Blocked because the only runnable scrollback proof uses scripted transcript data instead of long/multiline output from the real SSH target required by the contract.

assertions.title: The user can return to the live bottom after inspecting older scrollback

steps.action: Inspect available scrollback-to-live coverage
steps.expected: A manual/live-bottom recovery flow should be runnable on the real terminal surface
steps.observed: Only scripted-controller scrollback/jump-to-live coverage is available; there is no manual or live-fixture proof of returning to a live bottom while new remote output arrives.

evidence.network: SSH fixture reachable at 10.0.2.2:3122 from emulator (adb readiness check fixture_exit:0)

assertions.issues: Blocked because the current executable surface lacks the manual/live remote-output proof required for this scrollback-to-live assertion.

assertions.title: Copy and paste work through the terminal surface, including multiline paste

steps.action: Inspect clipboard coverage in the live-fixture suite
steps.expected: Copy plus multiline paste should both be proven
steps.observed: The live-fixture suite proves a clipboard paste action that yields remote output, but it does not prove terminal copy-out or multiline paste ordering/character preservation.

evidence.network: SSH fixture reachable at 10.0.2.2:3122 from emulator (adb readiness check fixture_exit:0)

assertions.issues: Blocked because runnable evidence covers only a single paste path and does not cover copy-out or multiline paste as required by the assertion.

assertions.title: The special-key bar delivers control-oriented keys needed for shell workflows

steps.action: Connect to the real SSH fixture and prepare raw byte readers/interruptible commands
steps.expected: The remote shell should be ready to observe control-oriented key effects
steps.observed: The live fixture session printed readiness markers for Tab, ArrowUp, and Ctrl+C checks.

steps.action: Use the visible special-key controls
steps.expected: Representative control keys should reach the remote shell correctly
steps.observed: Pressing Tab produced byte 9, ArrowUp produced bytes 27 91 65, and Ctrl+C interrupted a waiting Python process with CTRL_C_RECOVERED.

steps.action: Return to the shell after each interaction
steps.expected: Shell workflows should remain usable after key delivery
steps.observed: Each key path completed against the live fixture and returned the session to a usable shell state.

evidence.network: SSH fixture reachable at 10.0.2.2:3122 from emulator (adb readiness check fixture_exit:0)

assertions.title: Opening and closing the soft keyboard keeps the terminal readable, focused, and interactive

steps.action: Inspect runnable keyboard-visibility coverage
steps.expected: A keyboard open/close flow should be exercised on the emulator
steps.observed: No available instrumentation test opens and dismisses the soft keyboard while interacting with the live terminal surface.

evidence.network: SSH fixture reachable at 10.0.2.2:3122 from emulator (adb readiness check fixture_exit:0)

assertions.issues: Blocked because the current runnable validation surface has no soft-keyboard visibility proof for the terminal.

steps.observed: The current suites do not rotate the active live terminal session on the shared emulator.

steps.expected: A background/resume flow should be exercised against the live session
steps.observed: No available instrumentation test backgrounds and resumes the real app during an active terminal session.

evidence.network: SSH fixture reachable at 10.0.2.2:3122 from emulator (adb readiness check fixture_exit:0)

assertions.issues: Blocked because the current executable validation surface does not perform background/resume testing for a live terminal session.

assertions.title: After disconnect, the terminal does not pretend that new input is still live

steps.action: Open a real fixture session and verify live shell proof
steps.expected: The terminal should begin from a live connected state
steps.observed: The session connected to 10.0.2.2:3122 and displayed the remote proof transcript.

steps.action: Tap Disconnect in the visible session UI
steps.expected: The terminal should become truthfully non-live
steps.observed: The UI moved to Disconnected. and rendered a disconnected truth banner.

steps.action: Inspect post-disconnect controls
steps.expected: Live input actions should be blocked or clearly non-live
steps.observed: The input field, send button, and paste button were all disabled after disconnect.

evidence.network: SSH fixture reachable at 10.0.2.2:3122 from emulator (adb readiness check fixture_exit:0)

assertions.title: Unexpected remote-side or network disconnects are surfaced quickly and stop live interaction

steps.action: Inspect runnable disconnect-failure coverage
steps.expected: A live remote/network drop should be induced and observed
steps.observed: The current suites do not induce a remote-side or network disconnect during an active live fixture session; the available reconnect-required proof is synthetic recovery-state coverage.

evidence.network: SSH fixture reachable at 10.0.2.2:3122 from emulator (adb readiness check fixture_exit:0)

assertions.issues: Blocked because no runnable test currently induces a real remote-side or network disconnect against the live fixture session.

assertions.title: Process death or activity recreation restores only a truthful session state

steps.action: Inspect runnable recovery coverage for live sessions
steps.expected: A process-death or activity-recreation proof should exist around an active or recently active real session
steps.observed: The available test proves reconnect-required UI state survives screen recreation, but it does not recreate or kill the app around an active/recent live fixture session.

evidence.network: SSH fixture reachable at 10.0.2.2:3122 from emulator (adb readiness check fixture_exit:0)

assertions.issues: Blocked because the current executable surface does not provide process-death or activity-recreation proof for a real live session.

assertions.title: Terminal resize changes propagate to the remote PTY on orientation or keyboard-driven viewport changes

steps.action: Connect to the live SSH fixture and read remote PTY size
steps.expected: The session should report an initial remote terminal size
steps.observed: The test captured SIZE_BEFORE from the remote shell via stty size.

steps.action: Apply a smaller terminal viewport and read remote PTY size again
steps.expected: The remote PTY should observe the resized dimensions
steps.observed: The test captured a different SIZE_AFTER value from the remote shell and asserted it matched the resized viewport rows/columns.

steps.action: Verify the resize remains tied to the terminal surface metrics
steps.expected: The resize should represent the terminal viewport rather than a fake local-only change
steps.observed: The test computed the viewport from live terminal metrics and the remote shell reflected the new dimensions.

evidence.network: SSH fixture reachable at 10.0.2.2:3122 from emulator (adb readiness check fixture_exit:0)

assertions.title: Basic full-screen terminal programs remain usable as real terminal content, not just plain line output

steps.action: Enter alternate-screen/full-screen terminal content on the live fixture session
steps.expected: The terminal should switch into a coherent full-screen state
steps.observed: The test sent alternate-screen control sequences and observed alternateScreenActive == true with a full-screen program active status.

steps.action: Allow the full-screen content to redraw and exit
steps.expected: The terminal should return cleanly to the normal shell transcript
steps.observed: The test observed alternateScreenActive == false and then FULL_SCREEN_DONE in the normal shell transcript.

steps.action: Confirm shell usability after leaving the full-screen state
steps.expected: The session should remain usable after full-screen content exits
steps.observed: The session returned to the live shell transcript after exiting the alternate-screen state.

evidence.network: SSH fixture reachable at 10.0.2.2:3122 from emulator (adb readiness check fixture_exit:0)

assertions.title: Editing an identity preserves or updates the real credential behavior correctly

steps.action: Inspect runnable identity-edit coverage against the real fixture
steps.expected: Metadata-only edits and secret replacement behavior should both be validated
steps.observed: The available real-fixture tests prove metadata-only rename edits preserve the stored password and reconnect successfully, but they do not replace the secret/key material and verify the next authentication behavior.

evidence.network: SSH fixture reachable at 10.0.2.2:3122 from emulator (adb readiness check fixture_exit:0)

assertions.issues: Blocked because secret-replacement behavior is not covered by the available runnable validation surface, so the full assertion cannot be claimed end to end.

assertions.title: Keystore-protected secrets remain usable after normal app restart

steps.action: Create a saved password identity and connect to the real SSH fixture
steps.expected: The saved secret should authenticate successfully before restart
steps.observed: The fixture connected successfully and saved trust/identity state.

steps.action: Relaunch/recreate the app container and visible activity shell
steps.expected: The saved identity should still be present after restart-like recovery
steps.observed: After recreate, the renamed identity remained present and secret material was still retrievable from storage.

steps.action: Reconnect using the previously saved identity
steps.expected: The existing secret should still authenticate without recreation
steps.observed: The relaunched session reconnected to 10.0.2.2:3122 without recreating the identity or secret.

evidence.network: SSH fixture reachable at 10.0.2.2:3122 from emulator (adb readiness check fixture_exit:0)

frictions.description: The connected Android XML output aggregates both classes into one testsuite entry, so per-test class coverage had to be cross-checked against the two generated HTML reports.
frictions.resolution: Used both the XML result file and the per-class HTML reports copied into the mission evidence directory.

blockers.description: Manual/lifecycle assertions requiring real rotation, background-resume, soft-keyboard visibility, unexpected live disconnect, or process-death proof do not currently have matching runnable coverage in the available shared-emulator instrumentation suites.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Reviewed both assigned instrumentation suites and executed the full focused connected test run; no runnable test in scope exercised those lifecycle paths on the shared emulator.

blockers.description: Some contract requirements have only partial executable proof today: visible typed terminal input, real-target long-output scrollback, copy plus multiline paste, and secret replacement after identity edit.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Mapped the available live-fixture and scripted-controller tests to each assertion and only marked pass where the runnable proof fully covered the contract requirement.

summary: Tested 19 assigned assertions through the available shared-emulator validation surface: 8 passed, 0 failed, 11 blocked, 0 skipped. Passed assertions were VAL-CONNECT-008, VAL-CONNECT-009, VAL-CONNECT-010, VAL-TERM-005, VAL-TERM-009, VAL-TERM-012, VAL-TERM-013, and VAL-IDENTITY-010.
